CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES, P.C., A CALIFORNIA PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES, P.C.'S MOTION FOR ORDER THAT THE TRUTH OF ANY MATTERS SPECIFIED IN THE REQUEST FOR ADMISSION BE DEEMED ADMITTED, MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING DEFENDANT TO RESPOND TO INTERROGATORIES AND DOCUMENT DEMAND IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED. DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO RESPOND WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO RESPOND TO PLAINTIFF'S FORM AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS ARE DEEMED ADMITTED. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,165, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jul-19-2010 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Prove-Up Hearing.
Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Lois Jones, CSR #3689.

Drexel A. Bradshaw, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Bradshaw & Associates, P.C. a California professional corporation.
Drexel A. Bradshaw was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendant Barbara Stringer.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was filed on March 16, 2010.
Doe defendants were dismissed today, July 19, 2010.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Bradshaw & Associates, P.C., a California professional corporation, and against defendant Barbara Stringer as follows:

Principal - $59,835.16.
Interest - $12,910.13.
Discovery Sanctions - $1,165.00.
Costs - $580.00.

Total - $74,490.29.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar May-18-2010 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion For Order That The Truth Of Any Matters Specified In The Request For Admission Be Deemed Admitted; Motion For Order Compelling Deft To Respond To Interrogatories And Document Production Demand And To Pay Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Drexel A. Bradshaw, Atty. for Plaintiff and moving party: Bradshaw & Associates
No appearances made by Defendant.

RULING: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFF BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES, P.C.'s Motion For Order That The Truth Of Any Matters Specified In The Request For Admission Be Deemed Admitted, Motion For Order Compelling Defendant To Respond To Interrogatories And Document Demand is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant is ordered to respond to Plaintiff's form and special interrogatories and request for production of documents within 15 days of notice of this order. Plaintiff's Requests For Admissions are deemed admitted. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $1,165, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order was signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.
